figures.caption: Table 3: Results on MSQA. Results are reported under the official setting and vision-only setting. T, I, O, and PC denote text, image, object proposals, and point cloud inputs, respectively. The green rows represents our methods, it shows that our method consistently improves overall performance across both settings.

Fig. 4: Qualitative results on Beacon3D. For each query, we show (top) the input question and RGB sequence, (middle) intermediate visual and geometric outputs, and (bottom) the predicted and ground-truth answers.

Fig. 2: The overall framework of MAG-3D. Given a question q and RGB observations I, the planning agent dynamically orchestrates expert agents for spatial grounding, geometric reasoning, and retrieves information from scene memory M as needed. The framework maintains explicit, inspectable intermediate results (e.g ., grounded instances and geometric results), which are then aggregated and summarized into the final answer a. The right panel summarizes the functional components of each agent.
